title: Designs
description: Graphic artefacts (social posts, stories, banners, slides) where the agent designs freely in HTML/CSS, one artboard per design at its exact pixel size. The design.html export (artboards + PNG download) is generated by the backend, which wraps, sanitises and sizes each artboard. The brand block carries the identity tokens the artboards should honour — exposed to the HTML as CSS variables.

title.description: Readable title of the document (e.g. 'Larkfield — September campaign').

date.description: Date of the last substantial update, YYYY-MM-DD.

status.description: Editorial state: 'draft' while refined in chat (citing designs by id), 'confirmed' once the user validated. Absent means draft.

brand.description: The identity tokens. Exposed inside every artboard as CSS variables (--brand-primary, --brand-accent, --brand-background, --brand-text) so the HTML can honour them without hardcoding hex values everywhere. Changing a colour here restyles the batch.

accent.description: Highlight/CTA colour, hex. Falls back to primary.

text.description: Main text colour on the ground, hex.

voice.description: One line on the tone the designs should carry (e.g. 'warm, local, no corporate speak'). Guidance for the agent, never rendered.

designs.description: The artefacts. Each is one artboard: the backend wraps `html` in a container at the exact pixel size of `format`, with overflow hidden.

id.description: Unique design identifier (e.g. 'post-opening'). Used to cite it in chat and to name the PNG.

format.description: Artboard size: 'ig-square' 1080×1080, 'ig-portrait' 1080×1350, 'ig-story' 1080×1920, 'li-landscape' 1200×627, 'custom' (then width and height are required).

width.description: Artboard width in px — only with format 'custom'.

height.description: Artboard height in px — only with format 'custom'.

title.description: Readable name shown in the viewer list (e.g. 'Opening announcement').

notes.description: Working note (intent, channel, publish date) — never rendered on the artboard.

html.description: The artboard content: HTML with inline <style> allowed, designed at the native pixel size of the format. Constraints enforced by the backend sanitiser: no <script>, no event handlers, no external resources (no http/https URLs in src/href/url()) — system font stacks and CSS-drawn or inline-SVG graphics only, so the PNG export stays faithful. The brand CSS variables (--brand-primary, --brand-accent, --brand-background, --brand-text) are defined on the artboard container.
